node.statement: Reverse-triangle bound (NO ||Phi||=1): ||Phi(a)|| >= (1-delta)||a|| for a in A. Since a in A=Im P, a=P(a) (def-near-fixed-algebra). Then Phi(a)-a = Phi(a)-P(a) = (Phi-P)(a), so ||Phi(a)-a|| = ||(Phi-P)(a)|| <= delta ||a|| where delta:=||P-Phi|| (GT-Pprops: operator-norm bound ||(P-Phi)(z)||<=delta||z||, here z=a; delta<=C eta). By the reverse triangle inequality for the order-unit norm (GT-orderunit-def, the order norm is a genuine norm): ||Phi(a)|| >= ||a|| - ||Phi(a)-a|| >= ||a|| - delta||a|| = (1-delta)||a||. No appeal to ||Phi||=1 or any Phi-contraction.
